x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the Queens DA's narrative content as structured resources (press releases, cases, cold cases, programs, resources, victim services) and adds submit_tip — the net-new inbound write workflow (tip / cold-case lead / FOIL request) that has no channel today. Drafted to be shareable across all five NYC borough DA offices.

server.name: io.nyc.queensda
server.title: Queens County District Attorney
server.description: Agent-native access to the Queens County District Attorney — press releases, public-record prosecution activity, the cold-case / unidentified-persons (NamUs) initiative, community programs, resources, and victim services, all structured from the office's WordPress content — plus the net-new ability to submit a tip, cold-case lead, or FOIL records request that has no online channel today.
server.instructions: Use find_press_releases to search the office's 1,200+ releases by kind, prosecution stage, date, or text, and get_press_release for one. find_cases returns PUBLIC-RECORD prosecution activity (arraignment/charges/indictment/court-case) structured from that prose — never sealed or non-public data. find_cold_cases is the highest-value read: it recovers NamUs case numbers, sex, age range, and date/location found from the unidentified-persons posts so a family or agent can actually query them. find_programs and find_community_resources cover diversion/youth/violence-prevention initiatives and public resources (FOIL, property release, helplines). find_victim_services lists survivor support (services, not people). submit_tip (net-new) creates an inbound submission — a crime tip, cold-case lead, case inquiry, or FOIL request; tips may be anonymous, FOIL requests generally are not. It mutates state and routes to the office — confirm the type and details with the user before submitting, and never fabricate case facts.

tools.name: find_press_releases
tools.title: Find Press Releases
tools.description: Search Queens DA press releases, statements, and announcements by kind, prosecution stage, date range, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_press_release
tools.title: Get Press Release
tools.description: Fetch a single Queens DA press release by ID.

tools.name: find_cases
tools.title: Find Cases
tools.description: Search PUBLIC-RECORD prosecution activity (arraignment, charges, indictment, court case, conviction) structured from the press-release stream. Public record only — no sealed or non-public data.

tools.name: get_case
tools.title: Get Case
tools.description: Fetch a single public-record case by ID, including charges, division, disposition, and the press releases that announced it.

tools.name: find_cold_cases
tools.title: Find Cold Cases
tools.description: Search Queens DA cold cases and unidentified/identified persons — NamUs case number, sex, estimated age range, date and location found — recovered from post titles and bodies. The highest-value structured read in this domain.

tools.name: find_programs
tools.title: Find Programs
tools.description: List Queens DA community programs and initiatives (diversion, youth, violence prevention, reentry, conviction integrity, special initiatives) by category or status.

tools.name: find_community_resources
tools.title: Find Community Resources
tools.description: List public resources the office points to — FOIL records requests, property release, Conviction Integrity Unit, helplines, and language/disability access plans.

tools.name: find_victim_services
tools.title: Find Victim Services
tools.description: List victim/witness support services (advocacy, safety planning, compensation, court accompaniment, helplines, interpreters). Describes services, not people.

tools.name: submit_tip
tools.title: Submit a Tip
tools.description: Create an inbound submission to the Queens DA — a crime tip, cold-case lead, case inquiry, or FOIL records request. NET-NEW workflow with no online channel today (only phone or a static contact page). Tips may be anonymous; FOIL requests generally require contact details. Mutates state and routes to the office — confirm the type and details with the user first and never fabricate case facts.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.
